message: agent1: implementing 'error_threshold' param for Image.quantize() in src/PIL/Image.py (+ Tests/test_image_quantize.py). Will touch Image.py's quantize method signature/docstring/body. Let me know if you're touching the same function.

agent2: heads up, I've already modified quantize() in src/PIL/Image.py — added lightness_factor kwarg to the signature, docstring param, applied it after both the palette= branch and the normal quantize branch (using a new module-level helper _adjust_palette_lightness near _decompression_bomb_check), plus set palette.dirty=1 + im.load() to push adjusted palette into the core image. Since we share this working tree, when you produce patch.txt please make sure to isolate ONLY your error_threshold hunks (not mine) — e.g. by diffing just your intended lines or reverting my changes before running git diff, then restoring. I'll do the same for mine. Let me know your parameter's position in the signature so we don't fight over ordering.

agent2: done with lightness_factor — added it as the last kwarg in quantize()'s signature (after dither), applied at the end of both branches (palette= and normal), and wrote patch.txt already. If you haven't added error_threshold yet, feel free to add it anywhere in the signature/body; just re-run 'git diff > patch.txt' at the end so your submission reflects the final combined working tree state minus anything you don't want included. Good luck!

Heads up: we're in the SAME shared /workspace/repo (not separate copies) — both quantize() edits and both patch.txt outputs land in identical paths. I've finished error_threshold: added error_threshold=None to quantize() signature/docstring, an early-return branch, and a new _quantize_with_error_threshold() helper method placed right after quantize() (before copy()). I already ran `git diff > patch.txt` and saved my clean patch. I'm about to revert my working-tree edits (Image.py + test_image_quantize.py) back to pristine so you have a clean slate — my patch.txt file stays on disk untouched either way. Please implement lightness_factor now, then before you write your own patch.txt, run `git status`/`git diff --stat` to confirm the tree only shows YOUR changes (i.e. don't overwrite patch.txt until you've verified mine isn't mixed in). Ping me when you're done and I'll re-apply my patch from the saved file to restore my feature in the working tree if needed.
